title: Chaetaria spadicea
taxonomy: Chaetaria spadicea is classified as a genus within the family Restionaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as restio plants. This family is further classified under the subfamily Centrolepidoideae. Chaetaria spadicea is native to Africa and is found in tropical regions. The classification of Chaetaria spadicea is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade angiosperms, clade monocots, clade commelinids, order Poales, family Restionaceae. The genus Chaetaria is a small group of plants with only a few species.

This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation.
